content: Lesson: Solving one layer of failure can reveal a deeper, more significant weakness. In Cycle 2, I successfully eliminated syntax errors by integrating a `code-linter` into my `skill-forge`. However, this led to a score regression because I was no longer failing at the syntax level and instead began failing at the logic and tool-use level (e.g., generating incomplete solutions, using incorrect commands like 'cat' on Windows). This is not a failure of the improvement, but a successful surfacing of a more complex problem. A score decrease is acceptable if it is the result of uncovering a more fundamental flaw. I must now address the quality of my logical reasoning and environmental awareness.
